firecrawlQueryPatterns: this day in history turning point 2026
empire rise and fall history documentary 2026

voice.engineLicense: Apache-2.0 (weights + inference package, self-hosted, no API dependency)
voice.voiceLicenseNote: Rated 'B' target-quality, highest overall grade among British male voices in Kokoro-82M's VOICES.md -- chosen for 'epic documentary narrator' register. am_fenrir (American, deep/authoritative) is the documented fallback if bm_george doesn't land on audition.
voice.note: Per research doc: CPU RTF (~0.47-0.51) is an extrapolation from third-party benchmarks, not measured on this machine -- see this run's VERIFICATION.md for the actually-measured synth time.

music.note: Copyright Risk Flag in research/channels/echoes-of-ages-topics-voice-music.md leaves trending-vs-royalty-free as an explicit operator decision (unblocked-video risk is worse on Shorts than long-form). Pipeline defaults to the safe (non-trending) path -- see pipeline/run_channel.py synthesize_music_bed() for what is actually wired (a placeholder synth pad) vs. still a stub pending real library sourcing.

sfx.source: @rse/soundfx (CC-0 / CC-BY-3.0), individual soundfx.d/*.mp3 files vendored locally, shared across channels

visualTokens.notes: All hex values above are the moodboard's own PIL-measured pixel samples (research/visual-reference/echoes-of-ages.md, Extracted Color Tokens) -- none invented. explanationBase = year-badge navy (#0C1A25, near-black). explanationBaseAlt = parchment map-paper base (#C3B2AA), used as the TimelineReveal beat's backdrop. infographicCardBg = map-water navy-teal (#1A3240). calmInsight.primary = territory-fill olive (#8B8B70). lightnessEnergy.primary = the reference's second (green) karaoke-highlight neon (#00FF20). tensionBias.primary = the EPIC HISTORY brand red (#D13832). captionEmphasis = the reference's primary (yellow) karaoke-highlight neon (#FDFC00) -- the schema only has one emphasis-color slot, so yellow was chosen as the default and green is exposed instead via the lightnessEnergy accent slot. Cream lettering (#D0BBA3) is used as both moodAccents secondaries and the timeline marker-label/year-badge-numeral color. #141414 caption-outline was not wired as a token (WebkitTextStroke in TimelineReveal.tsx approximates it inline instead of adding a new token for a single stroke color).

typography.captionStyle: heavy condensed all-caps grotesque sans, white fill, thick black stroke + soft drop shadow (common 'auto-caption burn-in' style, not a bespoke typeface -- moodboard Open Gap: exact family unconfirmed)
typography.captionRevealUnit: 2-3 word caption cards, ~0.5-0.75s cadence (measured directly from a fps=4 ffmpeg sample of the reference clip)
typography.emphasisTier: two-tier: white default, one color-punched keyword per caption card (yellow #FDFC00 wired as the default emphasis token; green #00FF20 available via the lightnessEnergy accent for TimelineReveal/InfoCard-style beats)

spacing.infographicReveal: staggered/incremental -- one timeline era marker or InfoCard trait per speech beat, not all-at-once
spacing.brollDensity: low: at most two active UI layers (brand accent + one caption/marker cluster) at any instant, large open negative-space field, matching the reference's 'map fills frame, logo pinned top, caption pinned lower-third' composition

pacing.referenceShotStructure: single continuous 94s shot in the reference clip (animated map pan/zoom, zero hard cuts) -- this pipeline's shared ExplainerComposition is Sequence/hard-cut based only (see src/remotion/ExplainerComposition.tsx), so this channel adapts the format into a small number of hard-cut beats rather than literally reproducing one unbroken shot for the full runtime. TimelineReveal's continuous playhead sweep approximates the reference's 'one continuous camera' feel within a single beat; it does not extend across beat boundaries. Documented deviation, not a full shot-structure match.

motionPersonality.motionIntensity: low-to-moderate, asymmetric: calm visual bed (playhead/marker motion, no bounce/overshoot) carrying a punchier caption layer
motionPersonality.easingNote: ease-in-out/ease-out only for camera-like motion (playhead sweep, marker reveal), matching the moodboard's 'no visible overshoot/bounce, reads as smooth easeInOut or linear' finding; avoid spring/backIn/anticipate presets
